address: 59 Goldspink Lane, Newcastle City Centre, NE2 1NQ
lead_profile_text: STL-SW — switching vacant property to short-let. 2-bed furnished flat owned outright (no mortgage). Currently vacant. Found Stayful through Google search — high-intent. Needs property upgrade before listing: smart TV, kitchen amenities, bathroom amenities, professional photography. Deal Analyser PDF is image-only — figures from Granola and Monday columns. Y1 net £18,487; Y2 net £23,287; long-let comparison £11,092 annually.
emotional_profile: Pragmatic and research-led — found Stayful via Google search showing active consideration. No financial pressure (no mortgage). Primary gate is upgrade cost research. High-intent frictionless conversion profile once upgrade costs are understood.
delay_trigger_phrases: wants to research upgrade costs before deciding
delay_timeline: ~2 weeks (upgrade cost research); follow-up call in ~2 weeks; offer expiry 23 June 2026

top_objections.text: Wants to research upgrade costs before making a decision
top_objections.zac_response: Zac committed to providing a property checklist detailing required upgrades and estimated costs. Upgrade list delivery accelerates the decision.

top_objections.text: Performance expectations during initial ramp period — Year 1 will be lower than full projections
top_objections.zac_response: Year 1 projected 20% lower during ramp. Year 2 full projection: £23,287 net. NE2 is an extremely strong Stayful market. Manchester case study: 35k net vs 32k projected — we tend to outperform.

top_objections.text: Upgrade requirements needed to reach Airbnb standard — smart TV, kitchen, bathroom amenities, photography
top_objections.zac_response: Checklist to be provided. Upgrade costs are one-time; payback within Year 1 given strong income projections.

top_questions.text: What income can 59 Goldspink Lane, NE2 generate?
top_questions.answer: £1,940/month net average. £167/night, 72% occupancy. Range: £1,216 (October) to £2,538 (April). Y1 net £18,487 (conservative ramp). Y2 net £23,287 at full performance. ~£1,000/month more than long-let (LTL annual £11,092).

top_questions.text: What upgrades does the property need before listing?
top_questions.answer: Smart TV, kitchen amenities, bathroom amenities, professional photography. Checklist to be provided by Zac.
Standard 15% + VAT. Reduced to 13% + VAT if agreement signed by 23 June 2026.

top_questions.text: How long before the property achieves full income projections?
top_questions.answer: Year 1 projected ~20% lower during ramp-up (70-90 reviews and 4.5-4.8 rating needed for full projections). Year 2 reaches full performance: £23,287 net.

top_questions.text: How does Stayful compare to managing it myself?
top_questions.answer: Manchester case study: 35k net actual vs 32k projected — Stayful delivered above projection. Full end-to-end service, nothing for owner to manage.

zac_income_intro: Based on 12 comparable Newcastle NE2 properties: £1,940/month net average, £167/night, 72% occupancy. Year 2 at full performance: £23,287 net. Approximately £1,000/month more than long-let.
